topic.what: Secure rate-distortion-perception tradeoffs under strong secrecy constraint with randomized distributed function computation
topic.how: Uses output statistics of random binning (OSRB) and strong coordination techniques to jointly minimize distortion, enforce perceptual realism via total variation constraint, and achieve negligible information leakage
topic.why_matters: Establishes fundamental information-theoretic limits for trustworthy learned compression systems requiring simultaneous perceptual quality maintenance and eavesdropper protection in neural image compression and semantic communication

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mutual information between reconstruction and transmitted index
characteristics.evidence: I(Y^n; S) ≤ ε for noiseless; I(Y^n; eZ^m) ≤ ε for noisy BC
characteristics.context: Strong secrecy definition requires leak < ε that vanishes as blocklength → ∞

characteristics.what_it_measures: Total variation distance between output distribution and source distribution
characteristics.context: Enforces reconstructed output statistics match source distribution

characteristics.what_it_measures: Communication rate reduction enabled by common randomness
characteristics.evidence: Binary BSC example shows 45-52% rate reduction with 40-87% common randomness increase at distortion 0.1
characteristics.context: Gain not attainable in standard rate-distortion formulations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Auxiliary variable alphabet size required for optimality
characteristics.evidence: Support lemma based bounds in Theorems 1-4
characteristics.context: Finite alphabet bounds established via standard information theory techniques
